overview: A midrange-combo hybrid that uses early disruption to stabilize, then assembles The One Ring + Sensei's Divining Top + Tezzeret/Cruel Captain to generate infinite mana and untap lands, out-resourcing opponents to win through value or combo finishes.
win_conditions: Assemble The One Ring + Sensei's Divining Top + Tezzeret/Cruel Captain to untap lands for infinite mana, then cast multiple threats or use Tezzeret's ability to mill/win.
Alternatively, win with a high-impact creature or combo finisher once the engine is online.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands, The One Ring, Sensei's Divining Top, Tezzeret, or Cruel Captain, plus early disruption (Thoughtseize/Lightning Bolt).
Prioritize hands that can cast The One Ring on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 4+ lands, only removal, or no early game plan.

early_game: Turn 1: Play land, cast Thoughtseize or The One Ring.
Turn 2: Play land, cast Sensei's Divining Top or Tezzeret/Cruel Captain.
Sequence lands to ensure blue/black/red/green mana for key spells.
Use Divining Top to dig for The One Ring or engine pieces.
mid_game: Preserve mana for protection and engine assembly.
Use Sensei's Divining Top to dig for key pieces without clogging your hand.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if you can stabilize with disruption.
Manage your graveyard carefully to avoid giving Dredge free targets while enabling Tezzeret/Cruel Captain recursion.
Commit threats only when you have protection or can answer their interaction.
Hold back if facing heavy sweepers or counters.
Prioritize keeping The One Ring + Top + Tezzeret/Cruel Captain engine intact over trading creatures.
late_game: Assemble The One Ring + Sensei's Divining Top + Tezzeret/Cruel Captain to untap lands for infinite mana, then cast multiple threats or use Tezzeret's ability to mill/win.
Alternatively, win with a high-impact creature or combo finisher once the engine is online.
Go all-in when you have the engine online and can protect it, or when the opponent is out of interaction.
Commit to casting multiple threats or using the engine to win before they can stabilize.

sequencing_tips: Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board too early, forgetting to sequence The One Ring + Top + Tezzeret correctly, not managing mana for protection, and failing to track opponent interaction.
Unique interactions: The One Ring + Sensei's Divining Top + Tezzeret/Cruel Captain can untap lands for infinite mana.
Watch out for graveyard hate vs Dredge, and always prioritize keeping your engine intact over trading creatures.

matchups.advice: Use The One Ring + Top + Tezzeret to cast spells in response, or use cheap spells to probe. Sequence around counters by playing around their main phase interaction and casting key spells when they're tapped out.
Use early disruption to dig for their ramp pieces, Lightning Bolt/Abrade to remove threats, and race or stabilize with your own board presence. Don't let them get ahead with mana rocks.

Use Thoughtseize to dig for removal, Lightning Bolt/Abrade to clear early threats, and stabilize with your own board presence. Don't overextend into combat; trade efficiently and stabilize before going wide.
Pressure with early threats, use The One Ring + Top + Tezzeret to out-value them, and win before they can stabilize. Don't let them draw you into a long game; force them to interact early.

Win the resource war by using Thoughtseize to disrupt their engine, prioritize keeping your own engine intact, and out-value them with better sequencing. Trade efficiently and don't overcommit to the board.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, sweepers, graveyard hate, and specific combo pieces.
Note when opponents tap out for threats vs. holding up interaction.
Track when they use removal on your engine pieces vs. threats.
